Patient with heart failure with reduced ejection fraction reported worsening orthopnea and leg edema after missing several doses of furosemide. Exam showed bibasilar crackles, and echocardiogram demonstrated reduced ejection fraction.
Computed tomography angiography showed acute pulmonary embolism with right heart strain. The patient was started on apixaban after venous duplex ultrasound confirmed acute deep vein thrombosis.

A patient with poorly controlled type 2 diabetes mellitus presented with foot ulcer drainage and exposed bone. Foot MRI was concerning for diabetic foot osteomyelitis, and bone biopsy was planned before narrowing antibiotics.

Electroencephalogram captured epileptiform discharges during evaluation for generalized tonic clonic seizure. Levetiracetam was started, and seizure precautions were continued because of persistent postictal confusion.
The patient had heat intolerance, fine tremor, and a suppressed TSH with elevated free thyroxine. Endocrinology recommended methimazole and thyroid uptake scan for suspected thyrotoxicosis.

A patient with plaque psoriasis had erythematous plaques on extensor surfaces with silvery scale. Joint pain raised concern for psoriatic arthritis, although no joint erosion was seen on the initial radiograph.

Coronary angiography demonstrated critical coronary stenosis after non ST elevation myocardial infarction. Heparin infusion was continued until percutaneous coronary intervention, and the team monitored for recurrent ischemia.
The patient denied pleuritic chest pain, but computed tomography angiography later showed segmental pulmonary embolus. There was no evidence of right heart strain on echocardiogram.

Researchers used hemoglobin A1c and medication exposure to identify poorly controlled type 2 diabetes mellitus in electronic health records. Elevated albumin creatinine ratio was associated with progressive renal disease during follow up.

A procedure note documented ultrasound guided venous access for central venous catheter placement. The right internal jugular central line was placed using sterile technique, and the team monitored for line associated infection.
Brain MRI showed acute infarct in the right MCA territory after sudden left sided weakness and facial droop. Aspirin and statin were started, but persistent neurologic deficit remained at discharge.

The cohort study compared patients receiving vancomycin for diabetic foot osteomyelitis with patients treated for soft tissue infection alone. Exposed bone and positive bone biopsy were associated with higher amputation risk.
Emergency department evaluation showed fever, dysuria, and flank pain. Urinalysis had nitrites and pyuria, urine culture grew Escherichia coli, and ceftriaxone was given for acute pyelonephritis.

Postoperative patient developed hypoxia with bibasilar infiltrates on chest radiograph. Sputum culture and elevated procalcitonin supported bacterial pneumonia, and oxygen therapy plus ceftriaxone were started.
The oncology note described invasive ductal breast carcinoma with estrogen receptor positive disease. She received lumpectomy followed by adjuvant radiation therapy and tamoxifen.

Rheumatology evaluated morning stiffness and symmetric MCP swelling. Positive rheumatoid factor and anti cyclic citrullinated peptide antibodies supported rheumatoid arthritis, and methotrexate was started.
He presented with hematemesis and melena after heavy NSAID use. Endoscopy found a bleeding gastric ulcer, and pantoprazole infusion was started before transfusion.

After total knee arthroplasty, calf swelling and elevated D-dimer prompted venous duplex ultrasound. Acute deep vein thrombosis was treated with apixaban, and pulmonary embolism symptoms were reviewed.
An ICU patient with septic shock required norepinephrine, broad spectrum antibiotics, and lactate monitoring. Blood cultures later grew methicillin resistant Staphylococcus aureus.
A child with wheezing and increased work of breathing had asthma exacerbation triggered by rhinovirus. Albuterol nebulizers, systemic corticosteroids, and pulse oximetry monitoring were ordered.

After intravenous contrast exposure, creatinine increased from baseline and urine output declined. Nephrology diagnosed acute kidney injury, reviewed urinalysis with granular casts, and recommended isotonic fluids while holding lisinopril.

Electrocardiogram showed ST elevation in the inferior leads with elevated troponin. Emergent coronary angiography found right coronary artery occlusion, and percutaneous coronary intervention with stent placement was performed.

A pregnant patient at 34 weeks developed severe hypertension, headache, and proteinuria. Obstetrics diagnosed preeclampsia with severe features and started magnesium sulfate before induction of labor.
The patient had microcytic anemia with low ferritin and positive fecal occult blood testing. Colonoscopy found a bleeding colon mass, and packed red blood cell transfusion was ordered.
A patient with chronic hepatitis C had elevated transaminases and thrombocytopenia. Liver ultrasound showed cirrhosis with ascites, and hepatology started spironolactone for volume management.
Blood glucose was 38 mg/dL after excess insulin use, and the patient was diaphoretic and confused. Dextrose was given for severe hypoglycemia, and continuous glucose monitoring was reviewed.

A patient with fever, neck stiffness, and photophobia underwent lumbar puncture. Cerebrospinal fluid showed neutrophilic pleocytosis and low glucose, so ceftriaxone and vancomycin were started for bacterial meningitis.

The oncology trial enrolled patients with EGFR mutated non small cell lung cancer. Osimertinib improved progression free survival compared with platinum chemotherapy in participants with brain metastases.
A patient with obstructive sleep apnea reported daytime somnolence and loud snoring. Polysomnography showed an elevated apnea hypopnea index, and continuous positive airway pressure was prescribed.
The patient developed urinary retention after spinal anesthesia. Bladder scan showed 800 mL post void residual, and a Foley catheter was inserted before starting tamsulosin.
A child with fever and barking cough had inspiratory stridor on exam. Neck radiograph suggested croup, and dexamethasone plus nebulized epinephrine were administered.

The transplant recipient developed rising creatinine and decreased tacrolimus trough levels after missed doses. Kidney biopsy showed acute cellular rejection, and high dose methylprednisolone was started.

A patient with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease had worsening dyspnea and wheezing after a viral illness. Spirometry history confirmed obstruction, oxygen saturation measurement was low, and prednisone plus azithromycin were prescribed for COPD exacerbation.

The patient had atrial fibrillation with rapid ventricular response. Metoprolol was given for rate control, warfarin was adjusted after an elevated INR, echocardiography was ordered, and cardioversion was discussed.
A patient with recurrent migraine reported photophobia, nausea, and unilateral headache. Computed tomography of head was negative, and sumatriptan relieved the acute attack.
Behavioral health evaluated major depressive disorder with insomnia and suicidal ideation. The patient completed a PHQ-9 questionnaire, started sertraline, and arranged close outpatient follow up.

A hospitalized patient developed alcohol withdrawal syndrome with tremor, seizure, and hallucinations concerning for delirium tremens. Lorazepam was administered, and thiamine was ordered before glucose-containing fluids.
Emergency responders found opioid overdose after fentanyl use with coma and respiratory depression. Naloxone improved ventilation, and the patient was monitored for recurrent sedation.

An adolescent with diabetic ketoacidosis had high blood glucose, serum ketones, metabolic acidosis, and low potassium after vomiting. Insulin infusion and potassium replacement were started in the intensive care unit.
Electrocardiogram showed peaked T waves during severe hyperkalemia. Calcium gluconate and sodium zirconium cyclosilicate were given, and nephrology arranged hemodialysis.
The patient developed hyponatremia from syndrome of inappropriate antidiuretic hormone secretion and then had seizures. Sodium was monitored every four hours, and fluid restriction was ordered.
Primary care diagnosed hypothyroidism after fatigue, elevated thyroid stimulating hormone, and low free thyroxine. Levothyroxine was started with repeat TSH testing planned.
After a bee sting, the patient developed anaphylaxis with urticaria, angioedema, and wheezing. Intramuscular epinephrine was administered before transfer to the emergency department.
An inpatient with bacterial pneumonia developed sepsis and hypotension. Blood culture was collected, procalcitonin measurement was elevated, and empiric vancomycin plus cefepime were started.
Exam showed lower leg cellulitis with a fluctuant abscess. Ultrasonography confirmed a fluid collection, incision and drainage was performed, and cephalexin was prescribed after discharge.
Surgery evaluated right lower quadrant pain with fever and leukocytosis. Computed tomography of abdomen showed appendicitis, and laparoscopic appendectomy was scheduled.
A patient had right upper quadrant pain, fever, gallstones, and elevated bilirubin. Ultrasound supported acute cholecystitis, and cholecystectomy was planned.
Heavy alcohol use preceded acute pancreatitis with severe abdominal pain and elevated lipase. The team diagnosed alcohol use disorder and started aggressive hydration and analgesia.
Gastroenterology saw gastroesophageal reflux disease with dysphagia despite omeprazole. Endoscopy showed Barrett esophagus, and long-term acid suppression was recommended.
A patient with Crohn disease developed diarrhea and perianal fistula. Colonoscopy showed active ileitis, and infliximab infusion was restarted.
Rheumatology followed systemic lupus erythematosus with new proteinuria. Kidney biopsy confirmed lupus nephritis, and hydroxychloroquine was continued.
The patient had podagra with swollen first metatarsophalangeal joint and elevated uric acid. Colchicine treated the acute gout flare, and allopurinol was restarted later.
Dual-energy X-ray absorptiometry showed osteoporosis after a vertebral compression fracture. Alendronate and vitamin D were recommended.
After a fall, an older adult had femoral neck fracture and delirium. Orthopedics performed open reduction internal fixation for the hip fracture.
Neurology evaluated Alzheimer disease with progressive memory loss and dementia. The Mini-Mental State Examination score declined, and donepezil was started.
Clinic documented Parkinson disease with resting tremor, bradykinesia, and gait disturbance. Levodopa dose was increased because freezing episodes worsened.
A patient with multiple sclerosis developed optic neuritis and paresthesia. Magnetic resonance imaging showed new demyelinating lesions, and methylprednisolone was given.
The HIV infection visit reviewed viral load, CD4 lymphocyte count, and adherence to tenofovir. Low CD4 count raised concern for Pneumocystis jirovecii pneumonia prophylaxis.
A patient with tuberculosis had hemoptysis and an abnormal chest X-ray. Isoniazid and rifampin were started while public health arranged directly observed therapy.
Polymerase chain reaction confirmed COVID-19 in a patient with hypoxia. Dexamethasone and remdesivir were started because oxygen requirement increased.
During influenza season, the patient developed fever, cough, myalgia, and a positive rapid test. Oseltamivir was prescribed because symptoms began yesterday.
A hiker had tick bite followed by erythema migrans and facial palsy. Lyme disease was diagnosed, and doxycycline was prescribed.
Urethritis testing was positive for gonorrhea and chlamydia infection. Ceftriaxone was administered in clinic, and azithromycin was prescribed for partner treatment.
Gynecology diagnosed pelvic inflammatory disease after pelvic pain and adnexal tenderness. Doxycycline was started, and ultrasound was ordered to exclude tubo-ovarian abscess.
Urology evaluated benign prostatic hyperplasia with urinary frequency and high post void residual urine volume. Tamsulosin was started, and prostate-specific antigen testing was reviewed.
A patient with vaginal bleeding and pelvic pain had rising beta-human chorionic gonadotropin without intrauterine pregnancy on pelvic ultrasound. Ectopic pregnancy was treated with methotrexate.
Ophthalmology followed diabetic retinopathy with reduced visual acuity and macular edema. Retinal examination showed neovascularization, and laser photocoagulation was performed.
An eye clinic visit found glaucoma with elevated intraocular pressure and optic nerve cupping. Timolol drops were prescribed, and visual field test was scheduled.
The patient had cataract with blurred vision despite new glasses. Phacoemulsification cataract extraction with intraocular lens implantation was performed.
A child with fever and ear pain had a bulging tympanic membrane. Otitis media was diagnosed, and amoxicillin was prescribed.
The patient had sinusitis with nasal congestion, rhinorrhea, and facial pain for two weeks. Amoxicillin clavulanate was prescribed after conservative therapy failed.
Pulmonary hypertension caused progressive dyspnea with tricuspid regurgitation on echocardiography. Right heart catheterization confirmed elevated pressures, and sildenafil was started.
Echocardiography showed severe aortic valve stenosis in a patient with syncope and systolic murmur. Cardiology recommended aortic valve replacement.
Fever and a new murmur raised concern for endocarditis. Blood culture grew Staphylococcus aureus, transesophageal echocardiography showed vegetation, and vancomycin was started.
Nephrolithiasis caused flank pain and hematuria, and computed tomography showed an obstructing ureteral stone. Tamsulosin was given while urology considered ureteral stent placement.
Endocrinology evaluated thyroid nodule with normal thyroid stimulating hormone. Thyroid ultrasound showed suspicious features, so fine needle aspiration biopsy was performed.
Adrenal insufficiency from Addison disease caused hypotension and hyperkalemia. Stress-dose hydrocortisone was administered before discharge teaching.
Celiac disease caused chronic diarrhea and weight loss with positive tissue transglutaminase antibody. A gluten-free diet improved symptoms.
The patient had vitamin B12 deficiency with paresthesia and elevated methylmalonic acid. Cyanocobalamin injections were started.
Sickle cell anemia caused vaso-occlusive crisis with severe pain. Hydroxyurea was continued after discharge.

After an intentional acetaminophen overdose, the patient had nausea, rising transaminases, and concern for acute liver failure. Toxicology started acetylcysteine and monitored the acetaminophen level.
A term newborn developed neonatal jaundice with rising bilirubin and hyperbilirubinemia neonatal on the second day of life. Phototherapy was started, and bilirubin measurement was repeated overnight.
A dental abscess caused facial swelling and severe tooth pain. Dentistry performed incision and drainage, and amoxicillin clavulanate was prescribed.
A scald injury caused a partial thickness second degree burn on the forearm. The wound was dressed with silver sulfadiazine, and tetanus vaccine status was updated.
Dermatology evaluated an irregular pigmented lesion, and skin biopsy showed cutaneous melanoma. Sentinel lymph node biopsy was planned before pembrolizumab treatment.
Hematology diagnosed acute myeloid leukemia after pancytopenia and circulating blasts. Bone marrow biopsy was performed, and induction chemotherapy with cytarabine was started.
Genetic testing confirmed cystic fibrosis in a child with chronic cough, pancreatic insufficiency, and an abnormal sweat chloride test. Ivacaftor was discussed because the mutation was responsive.
After delivery, postpartum hemorrhage occurred because of uterine atony. Oxytocin was administered, uterine massage continued, and blood transfusion was prepared.
Vascular clinic evaluated peripheral arterial disease with exertional calf claudication. Ankle brachial index was abnormal, and cilostazol was prescribed along with smoking cessation counseling.
Psychiatry treated bipolar disorder after a manic episode with decreased sleep and pressured speech. Lithium was restarted, and valproate was considered if mania persisted.
A toddler with pica had lead poisoning after an elevated blood lead level. Public health inspected the home, and chelation therapy was arranged.
Audiology evaluated progressive hearing loss with abnormal audiometry. The patient was referred for cochlear implant assessment after hearing aids provided limited benefit.
Wound care assessed a sacral pressure ulcer with necrotic tissue and surrounding erythema. Wound debridement was performed, and pressure offloading was reinforced.
The ICU team managed acute respiratory distress syndrome with hypoxemia despite mechanical ventilation. Prone positioning improved oxygenation, and low tidal volume ventilation was continued.
Hepatology suspected autoimmune hepatitis after elevated transaminases, positive antinuclear antibodies, and positive smooth muscle antibody. Prednisone was started while liver biopsy was reviewed.
Gynecology evaluated chronic pelvic pain and dysmenorrhea from suspected endometriosis. Diagnostic laparoscopy identified peritoneal implants, and hormonal suppression was discussed.

During admission for acute decompensated heart failure with reduced ejection fraction, the patient developed rising creatinine after intravenous furosemide and contrast CT angiography performed for suspected pulmonary embolism. Brain natriuretic peptide was elevated, echocardiography showed an ejection fraction near 25 percent, and renal ultrasound showed no hydronephrosis. Lisinopril was held while spironolactone and empagliflozin were discussed for guideline-directed therapy after acute kidney injury improved.

Pathology from a lung biopsy showed non small cell lung adenocarcinoma with an EGFR exon 19 deletion, high PD-L1 expression, and no ALK rearrangement. Staging MRI demonstrated small brain metastases, but the patient had no seizures or focal neurologic deficit. Oncology started osimertinib instead of platinum chemotherapy and planned surveillance imaging to assess progression free survival.

After recent clindamycin and ceftriaxone exposure for cellulitis, the patient reported ten watery stools daily with cramping abdominal pain, leukocytosis, and a rising creatinine. Stool nucleic acid testing was positive for Clostridioides difficile toxin gene, while ova and parasite testing was negative. The team diagnosed antibiotic associated diarrhea from C. difficile infection, started oral vancomycin, and avoided loperamide because ileus and toxic megacolon were concerns.

A pregnant patient with systemic lupus erythematosus and prior lupus nephritis presented at 31 weeks with severe range blood pressure, headache, edema, and worsening proteinuria. Complement C3 was low and double stranded DNA antibody was positive, raising concern for a lupus flare, but thrombocytopenia and elevated liver enzymes favored preeclampsia with severe features. Magnesium sulfate and labetalol were started while nephrology and obstetrics debated renal biopsy versus delivery.

A patient with acute ischemic stroke arrived with acute aphasia, right facial droop, neglect, and left gaze preference after being found down at home. Noncontrast head CT showed no hemorrhage, CT angiography showed a left middle cerebral artery large vessel occlusion, and CT perfusion demonstrated salvageable penumbra. Tenecteplase was administered before mechanical thrombectomy, and later telemetry revealed atrial fibrillation requiring anticoagulation with apixaban.

The ICU team treated COVID-19 pneumonia complicated by acute respiratory distress syndrome, septic shock, and acute kidney injury. The patient required low tidal volume mechanical ventilation, prone positioning, norepinephrine, lactate monitoring, and broad spectrum antibiotics while blood cultures were pending. When cultures grew methicillin resistant Staphylococcus aureus, vancomycin was continued and remdesivir was stopped after liver transaminases increased.

After percutaneous coronary intervention with drug eluting stent placement, pharmacogenomic testing showed the patient was a CYP2C19 poor metabolizer with reduced clopidogrel activation. Cardiology switched from clopidogrel to ticagrelor, while infectious disease separately reviewed HIV therapy and avoided abacavir because HLA-B*5701 screening showed hypersensitivity reaction risk. The note emphasized that the pharmacogenomic findings predicted drug response rather than a new diagnosis.

In a randomized placebo controlled trial of adults with type 2 diabetes and chronic kidney disease, semaglutide reduced hemoglobin A1c and body weight while investigators monitored albuminuria, estimated glomerular filtration rate, nausea, and severe hypoglycemia as safety outcomes.

A multicenter cohort study of Crohn disease used colonoscopy, fecal calprotectin, and magnetic resonance enterography to classify active ileitis and perianal fistula before comparing infliximab dose escalation with usual care.

A systematic review of immune checkpoint inhibitor toxicity found that pembrolizumab and nivolumab were associated with immune mediated colitis, watery diarrhea, and corticosteroid treatment; colonoscopy was commonly used to confirm colitis severity.

A pharmacogenomics manuscript reported that CYP2C19 loss of function alleles and poor metabolizer status predicted reduced clopidogrel activation after percutaneous coronary intervention; switching to ticagrelor reduced recurrent ischemic events in the high risk subgroup.

A narrative review described status migrainosus as a disabling migraine attack lasting longer than 72 hours and summarized acute treatment with subcutaneous sumatriptan, nonsteroidal anti inflammatory drugs, acetaminophen, magnesium sulfate, corticosteroids, and dihydroergotamine.

A phase 3 oncology abstract enrolled patients with EGFR mutated non small cell lung cancer and untreated brain metastases. Osimertinib improved progression free survival compared with platinum chemotherapy, while subgroup analyses reported benefit in participants with exon 19 deletion and no baseline seizures.
ED note: pt w/ SOB and pleuritic CP after a long flight. CTA chest showed segmental PE, RLE venous duplex showed DVT, and apixaban was started before discharge.
Clinic note has misspellings: fevr with dysuria and flank pain, UA with nitrtes and pyuria, urine cx grew E coli, and ceftriaxone was given for presumed acute pyelonephrits.
Neurology follow up for MS documented optic neuritis, paresthesias, and new demyelinating lesions on MRI. Methylprednisolone was given; the note explicitly says no mitral stenosis.
Rheumatology note abbreviated RA with MCP swelling, positive RF, anti CCP antibody, and morning stiffness. Methotrexate was started, and the patient did not have right atrial enlargement.

Triage used the word cold ambiguously: the left foot was cold with absent pulses and severe calf pain, not a common cold. Angiography was ordered, heparin was started, and vascular surgery planned thrombectomy for acute limb ischemia.
Anticoag clinic note with typos: afib on warfrin, supratherapeutic INR, epistaxsis after dose change, vitamin K given, and repeat INR testing planned.
blue-zone bounceback (BZB): ED note says pt returned 36 hr after dc with SOB and pleuritic CP. CTA was neg for PE, but RN tagged BZB because med access and f/u barriers were unresolved.
triage-halo delay (THD): Urgent care documented fever, ST, and tachycardia, but the EHR banner made the visit look lower acuity. The MA wrote THD in the handoff so the ED team repeated vitals.
quiet-vent drift (QVD): ICU RT noticed rising FiO2 and subtle PEEP changes overnight without a clear desat event. ABG and CXR were ordered after QVD was discussed on rounds.
renal-watch window (RWW): Nephrology used RWW for the 48 hr period after IV contrast in a pt with CKD, ACEi use, and borderline K. BMP and I&O were trended q12h.
amber-fluid flag (AFF): The LP note described xanthochromic CSF as an AFF even though RBC count was falling across tubes. Neuro requested CT head and repeat exam.
nebulizer-rebound pattern (NRP): Peds asthma pt improved after albuterol neb but wheezed again before the next q4h dose. RT documented NRP and recommended closer pulse ox checks.
gait-freeze cluster (GFC): PT saw three freezing episodes during a hallway walk in a pt with PD and orthostatic sx. The rehab note used GFC to justify walker training and med-timing review.
sleeve-line pain (SLP): A dialysis pt reported burning over the AVF compression sleeve but no erythema or thrill loss. The RN labeled SLP and asked vascular access clinic to check fit.
dark-room nausea score (DRNS): Migraine clinic used DRNS when photophobia and nausea improved only after lights were off. The pt had nl head CT and responded to SQ sumatriptan.
insulin-fridge gap (IFG): Endocrine note says pt stored basal insulin in a broken dorm fridge and had fasting BG swings. CDE documented IFG and reviewed pen storage and CGM alarms.
home-O2 cord hazard (HOC): COPD pt tripped over concentrator tubing while walking to BR. PT marked HOC, checked SpO2 on 2 L NC, and requested a shorter cord path.
wound-photo mismatch (WPM): Wound clinic saw cellulitis improving on exam, but uploaded photos looked worse due to bad lighting. The RN added WPM before changing abx.
after-visit med fog (AVMF): Primary care noted the pt could recite dx but not which meds changed after dc. PharmD flagged AVMF and rebuilt the med list with pill images.
dialysis-ride failure (DRF): ESRD pt missed HD because the ride vendor cancelled twice. Social work logged DRF after K rose and SOB developed before the next chair time.
brittle-meal swing (BMS): Diabetes educator used BMS for glucose spikes after skipped lunch followed by large dinner. CGM showed hyperglycemia overnight and low BG before breakfast.
clinic-door panic (CDP): Behavioral health documented panic sx only at clinic entry, with normal HR at home. The MA wrote CDP after the pt aborted PHQ-9 screening.
night-splint refusal (NSR): Ortho hand follow up says the pt with CTS removed the wrist splint nightly due to numbness. OT marked NSR and refit the brace.
cough-loop revisit (CLR): Pulmonary clinic used CLR for a pt repeatedly returning for cough after viral URI despite nl CXR and neg COVID PCR. Inhaler technique and GERD triggers were reviewed.
tube-feed pause burden (TFPB): Nutrition documented frequent TF holds for imaging and PT, causing missed calories. ICU rounds used TFPB when adjusting enteral feeding goals.
blue-sock fall signal (BSFS): Nursing unit used BSFS when the pt wore fall-risk socks but the bed alarm was off. PT reinforced walker use after near fall to BR.
vancomycin-timing tangle (VTT): ID pharmacy noted trough drawn after the dose started, making the level unusable. VTT was entered before repeating vanc level and checking Cr.
chemo-chair crash (CCC): Oncology infusion note says the pt became pale and dizzy 20 min after premeds, before chemo started. RN marked CCC and checked BP, ECG, and BG.
rash-photo lag (RPL): Derm e-consult noted the rash was gone by visit time but still visible in portal photos from 4 days earlier. RPL was used before deciding against steroids.
pacer-card absence (PCA): ED could see a pacemaker scar but the pt did not have a device card. PCA was documented while cardiology called the vendor before MRI.
oxygen-tank anxiety (OTA): Pulm rehab note says the pt avoided leaving home due to fear the portable O2 tank would empty. OTA triggered education on flow rate and backup tanks.
transplant-call fatigue (TCF): Kidney transplant coordinator documented missed lab calls after tacro dose changes. TCF was added because the pt ignored unknown numbers during work shifts.
walker-fit mismatch (WFM): PT found the borrowed walker was too tall, worsening shoulder pain and gait instability. WFM was listed before ordering a correctly sized DME walker.
seizure-aura diary gap (SADG): Neurology asked for aura timing, but the pt recorded only seizures and missed the warning sx. SADG was noted before changing levetiracetam.
migraine-screen blink (MSB): The tablet MIDAS screen timed out during photophobia, so answers were incomplete. Clinic staff wrote MSB and finished the HA history verbally.
COPD-inhaler maze (CIM): Pt used ICS, LABA, LAMA, and rescue MDI in the wrong order. PharmD labeled CIM and simplified the COPD action plan.
statin-start hesitation (SSH): Cardiology recommended high-intensity statin after NSTEMI, but pt delayed because of prior myalgias. SSH was documented before trialing low-dose rosuvastatin.
bladder-scan surprise (BSS): Post-op RN found PVR 900 mL despite pt denying urgency. Urology noted BSS and placed Foley after spinal anesthesia.
post-ICU sleep crash (PISC): ICU survivor reported sleeping 16 hr daily after dc with nightmares and weakness. Clinic used PISC while screening for depression and critical illness neuropathy.
lactation-pump pain loop (LPPL): OB note says nipple pain led to less pumping, engorgement, and worse pain. LC documented LPPL and adjusted flange size.
methotrexate-refill knot (MRK): Rheum pt with RA missed MTX because pharmacy required a new CBC. MRK was added before sending standing lab orders.
foot-ulcer shoe gap (FUSG): Podiatry saw a diabetic foot ulcer healing in clinic but reopening after work shifts. FUSG triggered offloading shoe replacement and work-note planning.
apixaban-copay cliff (ACC): Anticoag clinic found the pt skipped Eliquis after the starter coupon ended. ACC was logged before switching coverage and checking for DVT sx.
bridge-clinic no-show (BCNS): Hospital follow-up team used BCNS when the CHF pt missed the temporary clinic visit after dc. RN called about weight gain and Lasix supply.
steroid-burst mood wobble (SBMW): Asthma pt reported insomnia and irritability after prednisone burst. PCP marked SBMW and reviewed warning signs for mania.
glucose-share blackout (GSB): Parent stopped receiving CGM alerts because the phone app logged out overnight. Endocrine documented GSB after child had BG 48 at school.
home-BP cuff drift (HBCD): HTN visit compared home cuff SBP 20 mmHg above clinic reading. MA labeled HBCD and recalibrated technique before med changes.
antibiotic-leftover start (ALS): Pt took old amox for sinus sx before visit, masking fever and culture results. ID note used ALS and reviewed abx stewardship.
mask-fit panic (MFP): Sleep clinic noted CPAP mask leaks triggered panic and early removal. RT documented MFP and tried nasal pillows with lower ramp pressure.
MRI-tunnel abort (MTA): Neuro ordered brain MRI for MS flare, but the pt stopped scan due to claustrophobia. MTA prompted anxiolytic premed and open MRI scheduling.
post-LP headache worry (PLHW): After LP for meningitis eval, pt called about positional HA and nausea. Neuro triage wrote PLHW and advised caffeine, fluids, and ED return precautions.
contrast-fear loop (CFL): Pt repeatedly cancelled CTA because of fear of kidney injury despite nl Cr. Radiology nurse logged CFL and arranged counseling before PE workup.
wound-vac alarm fatigue (WVAF): Home health noted the wound VAC beeped nightly until the pt turned it off. WVAF was flagged before checking seal, battery, and drainage.
nausea-pill stacking (NPS): Oncology pt took ondansetron, prochlorperazine, and leftover promethazine together after chemo. RN marked NPS and reviewed antiemetic schedule.
chemo-mouth shock (CMS): Pt starting 5-FU reported unexpected metallic taste and mouth soreness. Infusion nurse documented CMS and started oral care instructions for mucositis prevention.
discharge-paper overload (DPO): Geriatrics noted the pt had five AVS packets and could not find the CHF salt restriction instructions. DPO triggered a brief med plan.
tele-visit signal crash (TVSC): Video visit for rash failed whenever the pt moved the phone close enough to focus. MA logged TVSC and converted to photo upload plus phone call.
dialysis-arm protect gap (DAPG): Inpatient phlebotomy nearly drew labs from the AVF arm despite bracelet. Nephrology documented DAPG and added a door sign.
Foley-bag trip risk (FBTR): Post-op pt ambulated with Foley tubing dragging below the walker. RN marked FBTR and clipped the bag before PT session.
colonoscopy-prep fail (CPF): GI note says pt drank only half the PEG prep and stool remained brown. CPF was entered before rescheduling colonoscopy with split prep teaching.
pillbox day-shift error (PDSE): Caregiver filled AM meds into PM slots after daylight savings change. PharmD noted PDSE when warfarin and metoprolol doses were missed.
nebulizer-cleaning gap (NCG): RT found the home neb cup had residue and no cleaning routine. NCG was documented after recurrent bronchitis sx and poor albuterol response.
blood-culture redraw spiral (BCRS): Sepsis workup had two contaminated BCx sets and repeated redraws delayed abx. Charge RN logged BCRS before sterile technique coaching.
sleep-study wire panic (SSWP): Pt removed PSG leads during the night because wires felt like restraints. Sleep lab noted SSWP and planned desensitization.
vaccine-card mismatch (VCM): Clinic record showed COVID booster, but the card listed a different date and lot. MA documented VCM before updating immunization hx.
rash-blanch confusion (RBCF): Triage could not tell whether petechiae blanched on video. RN marked RBCF and sent pt for in-person CBC and exam.
prone-turn shoulder alarm (PTSA): ARDS pt developed shoulder pain during prone positioning despite stable SpO2. ICU team used PTSA and adjusted padding before the next turn.
home-hospice med lock (HHML): Hospice nurse found morphine comfort meds locked in a box only the son could open. HHML was logged before teaching the caregiver backup plan.
insulin-pen click doubt (IPCD): Pt was unsure whether the insulin pen delivered after hearing fewer clicks. CDE documented IPCD and practiced injection technique.
spacer-device loss (SDL): Child with asthma had albuterol MDI but lost the spacer at school. Peds note marked SDL and supplied a replacement spacer.
CPAP-water confusion (CWC): Sleep pt filled the CPAP humidifier with tap water and reported bad smell. RT wrote CWC and reviewed distilled water use.
opioid-lockbox gap (OLG): Pain clinic found oxycodone stored in an unlocked kitchen drawer with children at home. OLG triggered naloxone teaching and lockbox referral.
stool-kit handoff miss (SKHM): GI ordered FIT, but the pt left without the kit. MA documented SKHM after the portal reminder bounced.
echo-schedule churn (ESC): Cardiology rescheduled TTE three times due to transport and work conflicts. ESC was used before arranging same-day labs and echo.
rehab-stair fear (RSF): After hip fx repair, pt walked well on flat ground but froze at stairs. PT marked RSF and added stair simulation before dc.
home-scale battery bias (HSBB): CHF pt reported stable weights, but the scale battery was low and reading 5 lb under clinic weight. RN logged HSBB.
urine-cup label worry (UCLW): Lab received a UA cup with smudged name after dysuria visit. MA documented UCLW and repeated clean-catch specimen.
wound-odor threshold (WOT): Home nurse smelled mild odor but saw no drainage or fever. Wound clinic used WOT to decide on earlier review without starting abx.
IV-pump beep burden (IPBB): Inpatient on heparin gtt slept poorly because pump alarms triggered every reposition. RN noted IPBB and changed IV site setup.
lab-portal shock (LPS): Pt saw positive ANA in portal before clinician call and feared lupus. Rheum triage documented LPS and explained low-titer result.
transplant-med color swap (TMCS): Pharmacy dispensed tacro capsules with a new color, and the pt skipped doses thinking they were wrong. Coordinator logged TMCS.
nebulizer-mask tug (NMT): Toddler removed the neb mask every few breaths during bronchiolitis treatment. RT documented NMT and switched to blow-by technique.
chemo-cold cap ache (CCCA): Pt using scalp cooling during taxane infusion had severe head pressure but wanted to continue. Infusion RN logged CCCA and adjusted timing.
seizure-rescue spray gap (SRSG): School had the seizure action plan but not the intranasal rescue med. Neurology noted SRSG and sent forms to the nurse.
foot-photo blur (FPB): Diabetes pt uploaded blurry plantar ulcer photos, making erythema impossible to assess. Wound RN marked FPB and requested a ruler photo.
tube-site tape burn (TSTB): PEG site looked clean, but adhesive caused a rectangular skin injury. Nutrition team wrote TSTB and changed dressing supplies.
stroke-word stumble watch (SWSW): Family noticed intermittent word-finding pauses after TIA workup with nl CT. Neuro documented SWSW and ordered MRI plus speech eval.
asthma-spacer school gap (SSG): Child had rescue inhaler at school but no spacer, so wheeze worsened after recess. Peds used SSG and updated the asthma action plan.
dental-antibiotic loop (DAL): Pt repeatedly received amox from urgent dental visits without definitive extraction. PCP noted DAL after diarrhea and C diff concern.
infusion-chair chill (ICC): Iron infusion pt developed shivering in the cold room but no rash, wheeze, or hypotension. RN labeled ICC rather than infusion reaction.
brace-skin crease (BSC): Ortho brace left a deep crease over edematous skin without ulceration. OT documented BSC and trimmed padding.
ostomy-pouch ballooning (OPB): Ostomy nurse noted gas filled the pouch overnight and broke the seal. OPB led to filter teaching and diet review.
hand-therapy putty pain (HTPP): After tendon repair, putty exercises caused sharp pain but no swelling. OT wrote HTPP and reduced resistance.
post-op ride scramble (PORS): Same-day surgery pt lost the arranged ride after anesthesia was given. PACU documented PORS and delayed dc until family arrived.
home-INR strip shortage (HISS): Warfarin pt could not test INR because mail-order strips were delayed. Anticoag clinic marked HISS and arranged lab draw.
derm-photo shade shift (DPSS): Mole photo looked darker under bathroom light than clinic dermoscopy. Dermatology noted DPSS before choosing short-interval follow up.
injection-site map gap (ISMG): Diabetes educator found lipohypertrophy because the pt rotated pens by memory only. ISMG triggered a paper injection map.
cataract-drop schedule knot (CDSK): Ophtho pt confused pred, NSAID, and abx eye drop timing after surgery. Tech documented CDSK and color-coded bottles.
feeding-pump night alarm (FPNA): Parent slept through enteral pump alarms and child missed overnight feeds. Nutrition noted FPNA and changed alarm volume.
grief-call overflow (GCO): Clinic phone notes showed repeated calls after spouse death with chest tightness but neg ECG. SW documented GCO and arranged bereavement support.
fall-mat shuffle hazard (FMSH): Inpatient pushed the floor mat aside to reach the commode, increasing fall risk. RN marked FMSH and moved the call bell.
portal-message cascade (PMC): Pt sent eight portal msgs about one rash, causing duplicate triage threads. MA documented PMC and consolidated advice.
suture-photo panic (SPP): Post-op pt uploaded a close-up of normal absorbable sutures and feared infection. Surgery nurse marked SPP after confirming no fever or drainage.
lymphedema-sleeve heat (LSH): Breast cancer survivor stopped compression sleeve use during hot weather and arm swelling worsened. PT noted LSH and discussed lighter fabric.
anticoag-green-card gap (AGCG): Pt carried an outdated warfarin card showing last month's dose. Anticoag clinic documented AGCG after INR was subtherapeutic.
home-nebulizer plug hunt (HNPH): Family could not find an outlet near the bed during nighttime wheeze. RT logged HNPH and suggested a safe extension setup.

PubMed PMID 41972887. Management strategy in aplastic anemia patients with HBV infection following intensive immunosuppressive therapy in Chinese cohort: prophylactic antiviral therapy may not be necessary with close monitoring. PURPOSE: Acquired aplastic anemia (AA) results from bone marrow failure, and intensive immunosuppressive therapy (IST) is a standard treatment for AA. However, the elimination of lymphocytes may lead to the reactivation of hepatitis B virus (HBV) in patients with HBV infection, and whether to administer antiviral drugs for prevention remains uncertain.

PubMed PMID 42159071. Monocyte exhaustion associated with systemic lupus erythematosus. Monocytes are major contributors to systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE) pathogenesis, modulating B- and T-cell autoreactivity through inflammatory cytokine secretion and disrupted immune clearance. Under prolonged inflammatory pressure, monocytes develop a pathogenic "exhausted" immune memory state defined by paradoxical proinflammatory and immunosuppressive gene expression and diminished immune effector functions.

PubMed PMID 41872676. Sex determines the natural killer cell-mediated immunity against pancreatic cancer. Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma (PDAC) ranks among the most aggressive malignancies and exhibits a higher prevalence and poorer prognosis in male patients. While both the importance of natural killer (NK) cells in anti-tumor immunity and sex differences in NK cell function are well established, the interplay between biological sex and NK cell-mediated responses in PDAC has not been systematically addressed.

PubMed PMID 41933285. Gut microbiota-associated nutritional-immune status predicts prognosis in postoperative NSCLC patients. BACKGROUND: Surgical resection is the primary treatment for non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) patients with stages I and II; however, the postoperative prognosis varies among individuals. The prognostic nutritional index (PNI) reflects the nutritional-immune status of patients, but its microbial determinants remain unclear. METHODS: PNI was analyzed in a cohort of 372 retrospective and 139 prospective NSCLC patients.

PubMed PMID 41860373. Case report: persistent anemia after eculizumab in paroxysmal nocturnal hemoglobinuria: non-dominantly active intravascular hemolysis. OBJECTIVES: Paroxysmal nocturnal hemoglobinuria (PNH) is a rare condition characterized by intravascular hemolysis (IVH), thrombosis, and organ damage. Although C5 complement inhibitors such as eculizumab have significantly improved the prognosis of patients with PNH, persistent anemia remains in some patients. METHODS: We report a patient with PNH who initially responded to eculizumab but experienced recurrent hemoglobin decrease and became transfusion-dependent after 24 weeks of treatment.

PubMed PMID 42015349. Real-world efficacy of Bruton tyrosine kinase inhibitor based maintenance therapy in diffuse large B-cell lymphoma: a multicenter retrospective cohort study with external historical control. INTRODUCTION: The selection of an optimal maintenance agent in diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (DLBCL) continues to pose a significant clinical challenge. This study aims to evaluate the prognostic impact of maintenance therapy (MT) in DLBCL. METHODS: We conducted a retrospective analysis of data from DLBCL patients undergoing first-line MT at four hospitals in Beijing between January 2019 and August 2024.

PubMed PMID 41688870. Vitamin D levels in relation to sexual steroids, sexual function, and quality of life in patients of an andrology outpatient clinic. OBJECTIVE: This study sought to delineate a putative synergistic interplay between vitamin D and testosterone and their conjoint associations with sexual function and quality of life in men presenting with symptoms of hypogonadism. METHODS: A retrospective cohort analysis was performed in men attending the andrological clinic of the University Hospital Munster (Germany) between 2016 and 2021.

PubMed PMID 41952487. Elevated circulating unsaturated fatty acids: A novel metabolic feature of coronary artery disease without traditional risk factors. Coronary artery disease (CAD) remains a major cause of global mortality. While established risk factors are targets of effective preventive interventions, a substantial proportion of CAD patients exhibit no conventional risk factors. To investigate potential metabolomic biomarkers of residual risk in such individuals, we conducted untargeted metabolomic profiling of serum from CAD patients lacking traditional risk factors using ultra-high-performance liquid chromatography-tandem mass spectrometry (UHPLC-MS/MS).

PubMed PMID 41693486. SII serves as an independent diagnostic indicator for GDM and is regulated by placental 5-HT/NF-B signaling. Gestational diabetes mellitus (GDM) poses a significant threat to perinatal health. Diagnosis based solely on glycemic parameters shows limited accuracy. The Systemic Immune-Inflammation Index (SII), a quantitative marker of inflammation, may improve diagnostic precision. This study analyzed clinical data stratified by GDM and obesity status to evaluate the diagnostic utility of SII and its underlying mechanisms involving placental NF-B and 5-hydroxytryptamine (5-HT) signaling.

PubMed PMID 41934169. Protocol flexibility in PCOS: a combination of controlled ovarian stimulation and endometrial preparation strategies shows no impact on live birth outcomes after frozen embryo transfer-a retrospective cohort study. OBJECTIVES: This retrospective study compares pregnancy outcomes in polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS) patients across different controlled ovarian stimulation (COS) protocols-specifically GnRH antagonist and GnRH agonist cycles-combined with various frozen embryo transfer(FET) preparation methods, such as hormone replacement therapy (HRT) and ovulatory cycles.

PubMed PMID 42033241. An oral-gut microbial metabolite links Fusobacterium nucleatum to aggravated myocardial ischemia-reperfusion injury. BACKGROUND: Myocardial ischemia-reperfusion injury (MIRI) remains a major complication. Fusobacterium nucleatum (F. nucleatum), an oral pathobiont associated with cardiometabolic disease, may influence host physiology by reshaping gut microbial function through an oral-gut axis. Whether such microbial interactions contribute to MIRI remains unclear.

PubMed PMID 42246934. The cysteine metabolic regulation through CSE-transsulfuration pathway is essential in iron-induced oxidative damage of heart. OBJECTIVES: Iron overload (IO) cardiomyopathy is a major cause of mortality in patients with iron overload disorders. This study investigates the role of cystathionine -lyase (CSE), a key enzyme in the transsulfuration pathway for cysteine and hydrogen sulfide (H2S) production, in iron-induced oxidative cardiac injury. METHODS: We investigated the effects of CSE on oxidative stress, metabolic dysregulation, and cardiac remodeling using in vivo mouse models of chronic iron overload and in vitro ferric citrate (FAC)-treated cardiomyocytes.

PubMed PMID 41810737. From safety to agency: experiences of self-admission among patients with diverse mental health needs. INTRODUCTION: Self-admission models allow patients to directly contact their psychiatric ward for brief inpatient care based on self-assessed need. To ensure equitable access across all psychiatric services, a broadly applicable, transdiagnostic model for self-admission was developed in Region Stockholm - Sweden's largest public healthcare provider.

PubMed PMID 42192510. IgA Vasculitis with necrotizing arteritis: a multicenter retrospective study from the French Vasculitis Study Group and systematic review of the literature. IgA vasculitis (IgAV) primarily affects small vessels, but rare cases with necrotizing arteritis (NA) raise questions about overlap with polyarteritis nodosa (PAN). To characterize IgAV with necrotizing arteritis (IgAV-NA) and compare its phenotype with classical IgAV and PAN. We performed a multicenter retrospective study combined with a systematic literature review (1990-2025).

PubMed PMID 42259351. Widespread circulation of Alongshan virus in Austria and serological evidence for infection in humans: a nationwide molecular and serological observational study. BACKGROUND: Alongshan virus, a segmented RNA virus in the Jingmenvirus group of flaviviruses, was first identified in 2017 in China in patients with tick-borne encephalitis-like illness. Alongshan virus has since been detected in ticks and mammals in several European countries. In this study, we aimed to characterise the temporal and geographical distribution of Alongshan virus in Austria through nationwide tick surveillance combined with serological and molecular screening in individuals with suspected tick-borne encephalitis or tick exposure.

PubMed PMID 41930414. Temporal trends in second primary malignancies among long-term survivors of multiple myeloma across treatment eras: a population-based analysis of the SEER database. OBJECTIVE: To evaluate temporal trends in second primary malignancy (SPM) risk among long-term multiple myeloma (MM) survivors across treatment eras. METHODS: This retrospective cohort study utilized the SEER 17 registries database (1975-2022). Patients with MM as first primary malignancy surviving 5 years were stratified into four treatment eras: Era 1 (2000, pre-novel agents), Era 2 (2001-2010, proteasome inhibitor/IMiD introduction), Era 3 (2011-2015, new-generation IMiDs), and Era 4 (2016+, daratumumab era).

PubMed PMID 42127954. Persistent spread of carbapenemase-producing Klebsiella pneumoniae in acute care hospitals in 36 European countries (the CCRE survey): a prospective, multicentre, cross-sectional, epidemiological, microbiological, and genomic surveillance study. BACKGROUND: Carbapenem-resistant Enterobacterales pose a substantial threat to patients and health-care systems. We conducted a survey of carbapenem-resistant and/or colistin-resistant Enterobacterales (CCRE survey) in 37 European countries to describe their occurrence, geographical distribution, and population dynamics and inform control policies.

PubMed PMID 41864830. Beyond the ICU: family resilience and emotional turmoil after intensive care - a qualitative study. BACKGROUND: During the COVID-19 pandemic, visitation restrictions in ICUs intensified psychological distress among family members, yet the long-term impact of psychosocial support on family resilience remains poorly understood. OBJECTIVE: To evaluate the experiences of family members during the COVID-19 pandemic, focusing on interactions with healthcare professionals and well-being 18th months after hospitalisation.

PubMed PMID 42058263. Endoscopic Thrombin Injection for Gastric Variceal Bleeding: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis of Observational and Trial Data. OBJECTIVE: The safety of endoscopic thrombin injection (ETI) for treating the bleeding of gastric varix (GV) in patients with portal hypertension requires further evaluation. This meta-analysis systematically reviews the available evidence on the efficacy and safety of ETI for GV bleeding. METHODS: Two researchers independently screened and extracted data from all relevant original articles published from database inception to May 2025.

PubMed PMID 41932848. AI-assisted learning: ChatGPT for anamnesis in women's health education. UNLABELLED: Artificial intelligence (AI) tools such as ChatGPT are rapidly being adopted in medical education, yet evidence regarding their impact on learners' history-taking (anamnesis) skills remains limited. This gap is particularly relevant in women's health education, where high-quality anamnesis is essential for addressing sensitive, context-dependent concerns and for ensuring the safety of two interrelated patients: mother and fetus.

PubMed PMID 41792626. Current treatment for erectile dysfunction: an umbrella review of systematic reviews and meta-analyses. OBJECTIVE: This umbrella review aimed to summarize and provide a general evaluation of the effectiveness of current treatments for erectile dysfunction (ED) and assess the quality of evidence. METHODS: This umbrella review available in PubMed, Web of Science, the Cochrane Library, and Embase, covering studies published up to October 2025, was conducted.

PubMed PMID 41588655. Aberrant expression of triggering receptor expressed on myeloid cell-1 is involved in the immunopathological mechanism of myasthenia gravis. Triggering receptor expressed on myeloid cells-1 (TREM-1) and TREM-2, cell surface receptors involved in innate and adaptive immunity, exist in soluble (sTREM-1 and sTREM-2) and membrane-bound forms, with the soluble form serve as biomarkers in many immune diseases. However, the role of TREM-1 and TREM-2 in myasthenia gravis (MG) remains unclear.

PubMed PMID 42140754. Five-year follow-up of the SABR-5 trial: primary toxicity analysis of stereotactic ablative radiotherapy for up to five oligometastases. BACKGROUND AND PURPOSE: SABR-5 was a provincially coordinated, single-arm phase II trial of SABR for patients with up to five extracranial metastases across all six BC Cancer centres. The primary objective was to prospectively quantify treatment-related toxicity in a population-based setting. This analysis reports extended follow-up and long-term toxicity outcomes.

PubMed PMID 41667397. Pre-existing -lactamase gene diversity is associated with lower risk of ESBL-producing Enterobacterales colonization in patients exposed to ceftriaxone. Exposure to broad-spectrum antibiotics, particularly to third-generation cephalosporins (3GC), increases the risk of colonization by extended-spectrum beta-lactamase-producing Enterobacterales (ESBL-E). While clinical risk factors for ESBL-E acquisition are well established, the role of the gut microbiome and resistome remains unclear.

PubMed PMID 42023591. Gut microbiota reshapes host energy metabolism to modulate depressive behaviors. Disturbances in energy metabolism are a key pathophysiological feature of major depressive disorder (MDD). The gut microbiota, as a critical regulator of host metabolism, may influence systemic energy homeostasis and contribute to depression. To investigate this, we performed a multi-omics analysis integrating targeted metabolomics and shotgun metagenomics on samples from 100 MDD patients and 68 healthy controls.
